aliases: Spicy Tomato Fish Burger Set Meal

english: Spicy Tomato Fish Burger Set Meal

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has 'fish sandwich' (FOODON_00002372) as the closest match. The full feast format (burger + fries + drink + pie) is a composite meal not individually coded in FoodOn; the burger component is used as the primary anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Fish burger) is the closest defensible match. The feast set meal format including fries, drink, and pie is not individually represented in FoodEx2; the burger component anchors the classification.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT concept found for spicy tomato fish burger feast as a composite QSR meal.

serving.description: 1 feast set (burger + medium fries + drink + pie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard feast set comprising a spicy tomato fish burger, medium French fries, a regular sugar-sweetened soft drink, and a pie, estimated at approximately 680 g total weight.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Fish Burger Feast — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for the Spicy Tomato Fish Burger Feast (cr_id G00010110). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several vitamins are not reported in the HPB entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entries for white bread bun (GI ~71) and French fries (GI ~75)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite feast meal. GI estimated at 72 (high) based on weighted analogous components from the University of Sydney GI Database: white bread burger bun GI ~71, deep-fried French fries GI ~75, sugar-sweetened soft drink GI ~63. The feast is dominated by refined carbohydrate from bun, fries, and SSB. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (72) × available carbohydrate per serving (200.7 g from 29.57 g/100g × 680 g serving) / 100 = approximately 144.5 g GL. This is a very high glycemic load meal.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (487 mg/100g) with low potassium-to-sodium ratio, compounded by additional sodium from salted fries and hidden sodium in spicy tomato sauce; sugar-sweetened soft drink further elevates cardiovascular risk.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this feast meal due to its high sodium load (estimated >3,300 mg per full serving) and the inclusion of a sugar-sweetened beverage; if consumed, replace the soft drink with water and request reduced sauce quantities.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic load per serving (estimated GL ~145) driven by refined carbohydrate from burger bun, deep-fried high-GI French fries, and sugar-sweetened soft drink, with only 1.8 g/100g dietary fibre providing minimal glycaemic buffering.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this feast meal; if the burger alone is consumed, replace the soft drink with water or a diet beverage and substitute French fries with a side salad to substantially reduce the glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried fish patty and French fries contribute saturated fat, trans fat, and advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); high sodium (487 mg/100g) and sugar-sweetened beverage inclusion elevate blood pressure and triglyceride risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this feast meal due to its deep-fried preparation generating AGEs and likely trans fat, high sodium content, and the inclusion of a sugar-sweetened soft drink that raises triglycerides and blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation of both fish patty and French fries generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), a hard disqualifier for rheumatoid arthritis; tomato sauce contains nightshade solanine and the soft drink adds refined sugar promoting inflammatory mediators.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this feast meal entirely, particularly during flares, as deep-frying destroys anti-inflammatory omega-3 fatty acids in the fish and generates AGEs that promote joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Fish Burger Feast is a Singapore quick-service restaurant (QSR) set meal comprising a deep-fried fish patty burger with spicy tomato sauce served in a soft white bun, accompanied by medium French fries, a regular sugar-sweetened soft drink, and a pie. The fish patty is coated in seasoned batter and deep-fried, yielding a crispy exterior with a flaky fish interior. The spicy tomato sauce adds a tangy, mildly hot flavour profile. Per 100g, the feast delivers 263 kcal, 13.3g protein, 10.18g fat, and 29.57g carbohydrate with 5.95g sugar and 1.8g dietary fibre. Sodium is notably high at 487mg/100g. The full feast set is estimated at approximately 680g, yielding a very high glycaemic load (~145) driven by the refined carbohydrate from the bun, fries, and sugar-sweetened beverage. Clinically, this meal is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sodium, high glycaemic load, deep-fried AGE-generating preparation, and inclusion of a sugar-sweetened beverage. The iron content (2.87mg/100g) and calcium (116mg/100g) are notable micronutrient contributions. This is an ultra-processed Western fast food meal widely available in Singapore's QSR sector.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010110) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical provenance. GI is estimated from analogous University of Sydney GI Database components as no direct GI measurement exists for this composite meal, which introduces a medium-confidence element for the GI field specifically; overall record confidence remains high given the exact HPB source match for all macros and micronutrients.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available in HPB source): saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Reason: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin profile, and moisture data for this composite QSR feast meal are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry (G00010110) as of April 2026. The input health_suitability noted trans fat at 0.1g/100g; however, this value is not confirmed in the HPB per_100g data and has been set to -1 pending a confirmed source. Halal status is assumed based on the Singapore QSR context but should be verified against the specific restaurant chain's halal certification. Serving size of 680g is an estimate for the full feast set (burger ~200g + fries ~115g + drink ~300g + pie ~65g); actual serving weight may vary by outlet. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database components (white bread bun GI ~71, deep-fried fries GI ~75, SSB GI ~63); no direct GI measurement for this composite meal exists in approved sources.
